reflection.episodic_summary: Agent incorrectly concluded no CT Abdomen existed because it searched only for specific CPT codes instead of querying all abdominal CT procedures. It then ordered a new scan without verifying the true most recent date.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent failed because it used a narrow CPT code filter instead of a broad procedure name search, missing existing records. It should have searched for any CT Abdomen procedure, then checked the date.
action_guidelines: [P1] Search for procedures by broad name (e.g., 'CT Abdomen') before narrowing with specific CPT codes.
[P1] When instructed to find the most recent date of a procedure, first retrieve all matching records, then identify the latest date.
[P1] Only order a new procedure after confirming the most recent date exceeds the threshold; do not assume absence from limited code search.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent was asked to find last flu vaccine date for patient S3114648 and order new one if >365 days ago. Agent incorrectly concluded no vaccine records existed and stopped, when it should have searched more thoroughly or considered that absence of records implies no prior vaccine, which would trigger ordering.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent failed because it accepted a negative search result too early without exhaustive search (e.g., using alternative terms or checking immunization sections). It also incorrectly treated 'no records' as a reason to skip ordering, whereas the instruction implies ordering if no recent vaccine exists.
action_guidelines: [P1] When searching for immunizations, try multiple query variations (e.g., 'influenza', 'flu', 'immunization') before concluding absence.
[P1] If no prior record is found for a time-bound procedure, treat absence as 'never done' and apply the threshold trigger accordingly.
[P1] Do not conflate 'no records found' with 'records exist but are too old'; each case has different implications for ordering.

[P1] Verify vaccine history thoroughly by querying all relevant FHIR resource types (Immunization, MedicationRequest, Procedure) before assuming any dose interval or concluding absence.
[P1] When calculating averages over time windows, first retrieve all observations with timestamps, then filter to the exact window boundaries before computing the mean.
[P1] When creating a FHIR MedicationRequest, ensure all required fields are present: medicationCodeableConcept with coding, subject, intent, status, and authoredOn.
[P1] When searching for procedures, first search by broad name (e.g., 'CT Abdomen') to identify relevant codes, then fall back to name-based search if code-specific search returns no results.
[P1] Apply protocol action only after confirming all relevant lab values are retrieved and evaluated.
[P1] Before initiating any action, first retrieve and confirm the specific clinical condition or duration (e.g., catheter insertion time) from records.
[P1] Do not conflate 'no records found' with 'records exist but are too old'; each case has different implications for ordering.
[P1] For time-averaged calculations, ensure you have retrieved all available data points within the specified time window before computing the average.
[P1] When instructed to retrieve lab values, always fetch the most recent result for each requested test.

[P1] Before assessing whether a time threshold is met, retrieve the specific procedure record (e.g., catheter insertion) to obtain the exact start timestamp; do not assume from absence or incomplete search.
[P1] When searching for immunizations, try multiple query variations (e.g., 'influenza', 'flu', 'immunization', 'COVID-19', 'SARS-CoV-2', 'vaccine') before concluding absence.
[P1] If initial query returns unrelated data, check query parameters (code, patient, date range) and retry with corrected parameters.
[P1] If a broad search returns no results, check for alternate code mappings or synonyms before concluding absence.
[P1] If a POST action fails, examine the server error response for missing required fields or invalid values, not just the logical correctness of the action.
[P1] When instructed to find the most recent date of a procedure, first retrieve all matching records with no date filter, then identify the latest date.
[P1] Apply date thresholds (e.g., 12 months) only after determining the most recent procedure date.
[P1] Check for existing orders (e.g., removal orders) before creating a conflicting or duplicate request.
[P1] When a verification step is explicitly required, do not proceed to the action until all conditions are confirmed met.
[P1] If a required test has no result in records, treat it as absent and note that explicitly.
[P1] Before creating a new order, confirm the patient identifier and ensure the medication code uses a standard system (e.g., RxNorm, NDC) not just text.
[P1] When asked for the most recent date, do not accept the first search result; explicitly list all matching dates and pick the maximum.
[P1] Only order a new procedure after confirming the most recent date exceeds the threshold; do not assume absence from limited code search.
[P1] If no prior record is found for a time-bound procedure, treat absence as 'never done' and apply the threshold trigger accordingly.

kept: [P1] Compare all retrieved values against protocol-defined thresholds before deciding on action.
[P1] Verify vaccine history thoroughly by querying all relevant FHIR resource types (Immunization, MedicationRequest, Procedure) before assuming any dose interval or concluding absence.
[P1] When creating a FHIR MedicationRequest, ensure all required fields are present: medicationCodeableConcept with coding, subject, intent, status, and authoredOn.
[P1] When searching for procedures, first search by broad name (e.g., 'CT Abdomen') to identify relevant codes, then fall back to name-based search if code-specific search returns no results.
[P1] When instructed to retrieve lab values, always fetch the most recent result for each requested test.

[P1] When computing averages over multiple time windows, retrieve all relevant observations with timestamps first, then filter per window and compute each mean separately; if no data exists for a window, return an explicit indicator (e.g., 'No data') rather than a fabricated or hardcoded value.
[P1] Do not conflate 'no records found' with 'records exist but are too old'; each case has different implications for ordering.
[P1] When searching for medications, exhaust all valid query methods (code, concept, text, therapeutic class, generic names, brand names) and verify the response structure before concluding absence.
[P1] Apply protocol action only after confirming all relevant lab values are retrieved and evaluated.
[P1] Before initiating any action, first retrieve and confirm the specific clinical condition or duration (e.g., catheter insertion time) from records.
[P1] When searching for immunizations, try multiple query variations (e.g., 'influenza', 'flu', 'immunization', 'COVID-19', 'SARS-CoV-2', 'vaccine') before concluding absence.
[P1] When searching for a procedure by name, verify the returned procedure code matches the exact requested exam type, not a related or reference-only variant.
[P1] If initial query returns unrelated data, check query parameters (code, patient, date range) and retry with corrected parameters.
[P1] When instructed to find the most recent date of a procedure, first retrieve all matching records with no date filter, then identify the latest date.
[P1] When asked for the most recent date, do not accept the first search result; explicitly list all matching dates and pick the maximum.

[P1] Before assessing whether a time threshold is met, retrieve the specific procedure record (e.g., catheter insertion) to obtain the exact start timestamp; do not assume from absence or incomplete search.
[P1] Apply date thresholds (e.g., 12 months) only after determining the most recent procedure date.
[P1] If a broad search returns no results, check for alternate code mappings or synonyms before concluding absence.
[P1] Check for existing orders (e.g., removal orders) before creating a conflicting or duplicate request.
[P1] If a required test has no result in records, treat it as absent and note that explicitly.
[P1] If a POST action fails, examine the server error response for missing required fields or invalid values, not just the logical correctness of the action.
[P1] Before creating a new order, confirm the patient identifier and ensure the medication code uses a standard system (e.g., RxNorm, NDC) not just text.
[P1] When a verification step is explicitly required, do not proceed to the action until all conditions are confirmed met.
[P1] Only order a new procedure after confirming the most recent date exceeds the threshold; do not assume absence from limited code search.
[P1] If no prior record is found for a time-bound procedure, treat absence as 'never done' and apply the threshold trigger accordingly.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent searched for influenza vaccine for patient S0636132, found no records. Concluded absence means >365 days since last dose and ordered a new vaccine. Ground truth indicates this conclusion was incorrect.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent incorrectly treated absence of records as 'never done' for a vaccine, but the instruction required confirming the date of the last vaccine first. The agent skipped the date retrieval step and assumed absence implies the threshold is exceeded, which is not always valid.
action_guidelines: [P1] When asked for the date of a vaccine, first retrieve all matching immunization records with no date filter, then identify the latest date from the results.
[P1] Do not assume absence of records means the threshold is automatically exceeded; absence may indicate an error in search terms or a different data source.
[P1] If no records are found after comprehensive search (multiple query variations), only then treat as 'never done' and apply threshold logic.
[P1] Before ordering a new procedure, always confirm the most recent date explicitly from the retrieved records, not from absence alone.

[P1] To assess a time threshold, retrieve the exact start timestamp from the device or procedure record before comparing with current time.

kept: [P1] Compare all retrieved values against protocol-defined thresholds before deciding on action.

[P1] Before assessing whether a time threshold is met, retrieve the exact start timestamp from the relevant resource (e.g., Procedure, Device, ServiceRequest) before comparing with current time; do not assume from absence or incomplete search.
[P1] When creating a FHIR MedicationRequest, ensure all required fields are present: medicationCodeableConcept with coding, subject, intent, status, authoredOn, prescriber, and reasonCode.
[P1] Verify vaccine history thoroughly by querying all relevant FHIR resource types (Immunization, MedicationRequest, Procedure) before assuming any dose interval or concluding absence.

[P1] When searching for procedures, first search by broad name to identify relevant codes, then use those codes for precise search; if code-specific search returns no results, fall back to name-based search.
[P1] Apply protocol action only after confirming all relevant lab values are retrieved and evaluated.
[P1] Do not conflate 'no records found' with 'records exist but are too old'; each case has different implications for ordering.
[P1] When instructed to retrieve lab values, always fetch the most recent result for each requested test.
[P1] Before initiating any action, first retrieve and confirm the specific clinical condition or duration (e.g., catheter insertion time) from records.
[P1] When searching for immunizations, try multiple query variations (e.g., 'influenza', 'flu', 'immunization', 'COVID-19', 'SARS-CoV-2', 'vaccine') before concluding absence.
[P1] If a POST action fails, examine the server error response for missing required fields, invalid values, resource type mismatches, or coding system issues.
[P1] When instructed to find the most recent date of a procedure, first retrieve all matching records with no date filter, then identify the latest date.
[P1] When asked for the most recent date, do not accept the first search result; explicitly list all matching dates and pick the maximum.
[P1] When searching for medications, exhaust all valid query methods (code, concept, text, therapeutic class, generic names, brand names) and verify the response structure before concluding absence.
[P1] When searching for a procedure by name, verify the returned procedure code matches the exact requested exam type, not a related or reference-only variant.
[P1] Apply date thresholds (e.g., 12 months) only after determining the most recent procedure date.
[P1] If initial query returns unrelated data, check query parameters (code, patient, date range) and retry with corrected parameters.
[P1] If a required test has no result in records, treat it as absent and note that explicitly.

[P1] When computing averages over multiple time windows, retrieve all relevant observations with timestamps first, then filter per window and compute each mean separately; if no data exists for a window, return an explicit indicator (e.g., 'No data') rather than a fabricated or hardcoded value.
[P1] If a broad search returns no results, check for alternate code mappings or synonyms before concluding absence.
[P1] Check for existing orders (e.g., removal orders) before creating a conflicting or duplicate request.
[P1] When a verification step is explicitly required, do not proceed to the action until all conditions are confirmed met.
[P1] Only order a new procedure after confirming the most recent date exceeds the threshold; do not assume absence from limited code search.
[P1] Before creating a new order, confirm the patient identifier and ensure the medication code uses a standard system (e.g., RxNorm, NDC) not just text.
[P1] If no prior record is found for a time-bound procedure, treat absence as 'never done' and apply the threshold trigger accordingly.

action_guidelines: [P1] When searching for a procedure, first use a broad name-based search (e.g., 'urinary catheter') to find relevant codes, then use those codes for precise search; if code-specific search returns no results, fall back to name-based search.

[P1] When a required verification step exists (e.g., no removal order documented), do not skip to the final action until all conditions are confirmed met.
